subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **1 cloth bag** for drawing gems
- **Gem tokens**: various colored crystals with different values
  - Amber (yellow) — $1
  - Amethyst (purple) — $2
  - Emerald (green) — $3
  - Sapphire (blue) — $4
  - Ruby (red) — $5
  - **Obsidian (black)** — worthless, causes busting
- **Action cards** (dealt to each player)
- **Player screens/carts** (to hide your collected gems)
- **Coin tokens** (for final scoring)
- **Round tracker**

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Place **all gem tokens** into the cloth bag and mix thoroughly.
2. Set the round tracker to **Day 1** (the game lasts 5 days).
3. Place coin tokens in a supply pile.
4. Shuffle the action card deck.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Each player takes a **player screen/mining cart** to hide gems behind.
2. Deal each player **2 action cards** from the deck.
3. Choose a starting player randomly.
4. You're ready to mine!

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
Quartz is a push-your-luck mining game played over **5 days** (rounds). Each day, miners draw gems from a shared bag, hoping to collect valuable crystals. But beware — drawing **obsidian** causes you to bust and lose all gems collected that day!

After 5 days, the player with the most money wins.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Each day proceeds in turns, going clockwise:

On your turn, you must do **one** of the following:

### 1. Draw a Gem
- Reach into the bag and draw **one gem** without looking.
- If it is a **colored gem**: Place it behind your screen. Your turn ends; play passes clockwise.
- If it is **obsidian**: You **bust**! You must leave the mine immediately. Return all gems you collected this day to the bag. You receive a **consolation prize of $2**. You take no more turns this day.
- If you draw a **second obsidian** on a different turn the same day, you also bust (each obsidian drawn is separate).

### 2. Leave the Mine Voluntarily
- You choose to stop mining for the day.
- **Sell all gems** you collected this day for their listed values.
- You take no more turns this day.

### 3. Play an Action Card
- Instead of drawing, play one action card from your hand for its effect.
- Action cards can do things like: peek at gems before drawing, force another player to draw, swap gems, protect from obsidian, steal gems, etc.
- After resolving the card, your turn ends.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
### Selling Gems
When you leave the mine (voluntarily or at day's end), sell your collected gems:
- Amber = $1, Amethyst = $2, Emerald = $3, Sapphire = $4, Ruby = $5
- Take coins from the supply equal to the total value.

### Action Cards
Action cards add interaction and strategy:
- **Peek**: Look at 2 gems in the bag before deciding to draw.
- **Swap**: Exchange a gem behind your screen with one from another player.
- **Shield**: Protect yourself — if you draw obsidian this turn, ignore it.
- **Sabotage**: Force another player to draw a gem on your turn.
- **Steal**: Take a gem from another player's collection.

You draw new action cards at the start of each new day.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
### Duplicate Obsidian Rule
If you draw obsidian, you bust and leave. Each obsidian is an independent bust trigger.

### End of a Day
A day ends when **all players** have either busted or voluntarily left the mine. Return all remaining gems to the bag, shuffle, and start the next day.

### Action Card Draw
At the start of each new day (Days 2-5), each player draws **1 new action card**.

### Last Player Standing
If you are the last player still in the mine, you may continue drawing gems (risk vs. reward with no competition).

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
After **Day 5** is completed, the game ends.

- Count up all coins earned across all 5 days.
- The player with the **most money** wins.
- In case of a tie, the tied player who earned more money on Day 5 wins.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Know when to leave**. If you've collected 2-3 good gems, leaving with guaranteed money is often better than risking a bust.
- **Count the obsidian**. The bag has a fixed number of obsidian tokens. If several have already been drawn, the bag is safer.
- **Save action cards** for critical moments — don't waste them early.
- **Watch other players**. If they're busting and returning gems to the bag, the gem distribution changes.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Bag counting**: Track how many obsidian tokens are left in the bag vs. total gems remaining. This tells you your bust probability.
- **Aggressive early, conservative late**: On Days 1-2, you can afford to push your luck. On Days 4-5, protecting your earnings matters more.
- **Action card timing**: Shield cards are best used when the bag is dangerous. Sabotage cards are best used when an opponent has a big haul.
- **Consolation awareness**: The $2 consolation for busting isn't nothing — sometimes a risky draw has good expected value even accounting for the bust.
